Website Terms

Introduction Welcome to PlusX.app (the "Website"). This is a PulseChain community-driven website interface to run code. By accessing or using this Website, you agree to comply with and be bound by these Terms of Use ("Terms"). If you do not agree with these Terms, please do not use the Website.

While PlusX website does not knowingly collect your data, however your device, browser, wallet, remote procedure call node and server operators, internet service and virtual private network providers likely do.

No Financial Advice Any information on this site is for educational and entertainment purposes only. Always consult a qualified financial professionals and use test wallets with before making transactions. PlusX.app does not provide financial, investment, or trading advice.

Below, you will find Risks in bold and Mitigation measures taken.

Risk of User Data Leaks uP only keeps essential operational data and keeps user data safe by not storing user data (metadata).

Risk of Not Having Enough Advertisers uP is tackling this by increasing the amount of evergreen content and onboarding influencers.

Risk of Unstable Money Flow into uP Chart uP has new features (collateralized buy and burn), staking yield, liquidity bonanza and more to help bring liquidity beyond community buys.

Risk from Competitors uP is making its smart contract ecosystem unique with community-driven uPdates and educational content.

Currency Risk The assets used in uP (Pulsechain or PLS) can change in value compared to the US dollar, which can be risky for those buying uP tokens or PLS.

Market Risk The value of uP's assets can go down at any time because of an open market (big wallets are welcome to sell at any time), which could lead to an increase or decrease in the worth of uP tokens—mitigated by uP strategy buys and burns from various sources.

Operational User Risk There's a risk of losing assets due to the user's inability to perform (for example burning tokens by mistake). To mitigate the risk - user centric design is implemented.

Tax Risk Different tax rules and changes in tax laws can lead to different results. The way capital gains are taxed under this contract can vary widely. Nothing can be done to mitigate your Tax Risk. Taxes are taxes.

Liquidation Risk Vaults (who give out loans) can be liquidated when ICR is 250% or redeemed at any time. Mitigation is uP strategy - by the time vault is liquidated, there are stability pool funds to open new vaults.

Infrastructure Risk, Including Custody of Admin Keys For example, there's a risk that token owners can't use the update button properly if the underlying technology fails. This includes problems with managing or moving assets due to technical glitches, bugs, or exploits. Mitigation is done with Admin keys and the user's ability to engage with public call functions of up-smart contracts.

Liquidity on the Secondary Market There's no official secondary market for uP. Trading positions might use automated market makers, which comes with risks and could lead to a loss. uP strategy exploits the risks to move the price up. Liquidity Bonanza Mitigates the risks.

Underlying Technology Updates Blockchain and other underlying technology updates used in uP smart contracts can ruin the work of uP smart contracts. Admin keys are there to mitigate the risk.

Market Sentiment Investor attitudes like fear, uncertainty, and doubt (fud) can greatly affect the price and availability of an asset. Things like news, social media, and public opinion can quickly change how the market feels, causing unpredictable price changes. Mitigation is to prepare yourself for volatility by understanding that market sentiment is an inherent part of trading in any financial market. uP can mitigate this by maintaining transparent communication, providing regular updates, and engaging with the community to build trust and confidence. Additionally, uP can offer educational resources to help users understand market dynamics and the potential impact of sentiment on prices. This approach can help stabilize user expectations and reduce panic-driven decisions.
